# SRC011 - A Comparison of Approaches to Advertising Measurement

## Metadata

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Section | Measurement |
| Authors / source | Gordon, Zettelmeyer, Bhargava & Chapsky |
| Year | 2018/2019 |
| Source type | Field experiments at Facebook / Marketing Science |
| Quality | High |
| URL | [https://www.kellogg.northwestern.edu/faculty/gordon_b/files/fb_comparison.pdf](https://www.kellogg.northwestern.edu/faculty/gordon_b/files/fb_comparison.pdf) |

## Bias / limitation

Uses Facebook experiments; platform context matters.

## Core takeaway

Observational measurement can diverge from randomized lift; granular data does not solve causal bias by itself.

## How the marketing agent should use it

Use to design holdouts and skepticism around platform attribution.

## Tactics unlocked

- Convert source insight into a campaign hypothesis.
- Translate the hypothesis into one ad angle, one landing-page proof section, and one measurement plan.
- Record whether the tactic worked in `memory/tactic_performance.md`.

## Agent instruction

When this source is relevant, write one sentence explaining **why this source applies to the buyer state** before using it.
